title: UK First Sea Lord credits Ukraine with degrading one-third of Russian Black Sea fleet
summary: Speaking at the Royal United Services Institute, UK First Sea Lord Sir Gwyn Jenkins assessed that Ukraine has destroyed or disabled approximately one-third of Russia's Black Sea Fleet using maritime drones, uncrewed surface vessels and long-range strikes, while emphasising the new JEF maritime force role complementing NATO.
contents:
# UK First Sea Lord credits Ukraine with degrading one-third of Russian Black Sea Fleet

## Speech Highlights
UK First Sea Lord Admiral Sir Gwyn Jenkins addressed the Royal United Services Institute (RUSI) on April 29-30 and assessed that Ukraine has destroyed or disabled roughly one-third of Russia's Black Sea Fleet through coordinated employment of maritime drones, uncrewed surface vessels (USVs), long-range cruise missile and ballistic missile strikes. Jenkins framed the achievement as a doctrinal turning point in modern naval warfare and validation of distributed, low-cost denial.

## Doctrinal Implications
Jenkins highlighted that asymmetric maritime denial proved capable of forcing a peer adversary's fleet from forward basing into deeper rear areas, with strategic effect comparable to traditional carrier-based sea control. He linked these lessons to the newly announced Joint Expeditionary Force (JEF) maritime formation, arguing that NATO and JEF members must invest in unmanned, distributed and long-range fires capabilities.

## Regional Outlook
Jenkins emphasised that the High North, North Atlantic and Baltic regions present the most immediate threat geometry, citing Russian Northern Fleet posture, undersea infrastructure threats and shadow-fleet operations. He confirmed JEF will train and operate together across these waters with potential command from Northwood.
